article_meta.title: Takeaways from the millions of newly released Epstein files 2 hours agoShareSaveSakshi VenkatramanandKwasi Gyamfi Asiedu

reasoning: The model returned an empty array for a lengthy article that contains several defensible instances of author-level bias. While the article is largely factual reporting, there are some notable author-prose choices worth flagging. For instance, 'controversial plea agreement' uses a subjective qualifying adjective, 'disgraced financier' is a loaded characterization in the author's own voice, 'putting more holes in Andrew's assurances' is interpretive spin, and 'Many - including within Trump's base - have long believed there was a conspiracy to protect the rich and powerful' could be seen as unsubstantiated framing. The article also arguably shows some slant in how it structures the information about different high-profile figures. However, this is a long, largely factual news article and many of these instances are borderline. The model correctly avoided flagging any quoted text, which is good.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: The model returned an empty array, so there are zero attribution rule violations. No quoted text was flagged.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: No items were flagged at all, so there are zero false positives.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The model missed several defensible bias instances in the author's own prose: (1) 'disgraced financier' - subjective qualifying adjective/word choice applied repeatedly; (2) 'controversial plea agreement' - subjective qualifying adjective; (3) 'putting more holes in Andrew's assurances' - spin/interpretive language in author's voice; (4) The structural emphasis on salacious details about various figures while burying the victims' concerns lower in the article could be flagged as slant. These are moderate misses - some are borderline but 'disgraced financier' and 'putting more holes in' are fairly clear author-voice bias.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: No bias types were applied, so there are no incorrect labels to evaluate.
